senses.gloss: flask with an elongate neck of usually glass for liquids

etymology.text: From Middle Persian [script needed] (dōrak, dōlak). Also found in Classical Persian دوره (dōra, “winejug, scyphus, a kind of flask for measure”), Old Armenian դորակ (dorak, “a kind of liquid measure”), Classical Syriac ܕܰܘܪܩܳܐ ܪܘܡܝܐ (dawrəqā rōmāyā, “amphora with handles; a kind of measure of 80 Roman litres”).

body.text: containing the quantity to be drunk
,] arabicized: (L, TA:) [J says,] I think it to be Persian, arabicized: (S:) it is thus correctly written; not, as the context of the K requires it to be in this sense,
A jar having a loop-shaped handle,
of the dial. of the people of Mekkeh: pl.
A narrownecked drinking-bottle, made of a dust-coloured, or grayish, porous earth, for the purpose of cooling the water by evaporation:
several varieties of this kind of bottle are figured in ch. v. of my “ Modern Egyptians. ”]
